title: [InstCombine] make `foldBinOpIntoSelectOrPhi` fold on all operands
author: imkiva
description:
This PR makes `foldBinOpIntoSelectOrPhi` fold to select/phi both for operands 0 and 1.

alive2: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/T56TMM
also fixes #183498

tests.test_body:
define i32 @sub_partial_fold_phi_operand0_phi_swapped_binop_swapped(i1 %which, i32 %x) {
entry:
  br i1 %which, label %var.in, label %const.in

var.in:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %x1 = add i32 %x, 1
  br label %final

const.in:                                         ; preds = %entry
  br label %final

final:                                            ; preds = %const.in, %var.in
  %p = phi i32 [ %x1, %var.in ], [ 10, %const.in ]
  %value = sub i32 %p, 123
  ret i32 %value
}

define i32 @sub_partial_fold_phi_operand0_binop_swapped(i1 %which, i32 %x) {
entry:
  br i1 %which, label %const.in, label %var.in

const.in:                                         ; preds = %entry
  br label %final

var.in:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %x1 = add i32 %x, 1
  br label %final

final:                                            ; preds = %var.in, %const.in
  %p = phi i32 [ 10, %const.in ], [ %x1, %var.in ]
  %value = sub i32 %p, 123
  ret i32 %value
}

define i32 @sub_partial_fold_phi_operand1(i1 %which, i32 %x) {
entry:
  br i1 %which, label %const.in, label %var.in

const.in:                                         ; preds = %entry
  br label %final

var.in:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %x1 = add i32 %x, 1
  br label %final

final:                                            ; preds = %var.in, %const.in
  %p = phi i32 [ 10, %const.in ], [ %x1, %var.in ]
  %value = sub i32 123, %p
  ret i32 %value
}

define i32 @sub_partial_fold_phi_operand1_phi_swapped(i1 %which, i32 %x) {
entry:
  br i1 %which, label %var.in, label %const.in

var.in:                                           ; preds = %entry
  %x1 = add i32 %x, 1
  br label %final

const.in:                                         ; preds = %entry
  br label %final

final:                                            ; preds = %const.in, %var.in
  %p = phi i32 [ %x1, %var.in ], [ 10, %const.in ]
  %value = sub i32 123, %p
  ret i32 %value
}

define <2 x i1> @pr173177(<2 x i1> %a, i1 %b) {
entry:
  %s1 = select <2 x i1> %a, <2 x i16> splat (i16 179), <2 x i16> splat (i16 1)
  %s2 = select i1 %b, <2 x i16> zeroinitializer, <2 x i16> splat (i16 255)
  %and = and <2 x i16> %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp eq <2 x i16> %and, zeroinitializer
  ret <2 x i1> %cmp
}

define <2 x i1> @icmp_eq_or_of_selects_with_constant_vectorized_nonsplat(<2 x i1> %a, <2 x i1> %b) {
  %s1 = select <2 x i1> %a, <2 x i64> splat (i64 65536), <2 x i64> zeroinitializer
  %s2 = select <2 x i1> %b, <2 x i64> <i64 256, i64 128>, <2 x i64> zeroinitializer
  %or = or <2 x i64> %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp eq <2 x i64> %or, <i64 65792, i64 65664>
  ret <2 x i1> %cmp
}

define i64 @test_and3(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %a, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv.next = and i64 %b, %iv
  tail call void @use(i64 %iv.next)
  br label %loop
}

declare void @use(i64)

define i64 @test_or3(i64 %a, i64 %b) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ %a, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %iv.next = or i64 %b, %iv
  tail call void @use(i64 %iv.next)
  br label %loop
}

declare void @use(i64)

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @pr61361(i32 %arg) {
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i32 %arg, 1
  %sel1 = select i1 %cmp1, i32 0, i32 33554431
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %arg, 0
  %sel2 = select i1 %cmp2, i32 %sel1, i32 0
  %ashr = ashr i32 %sel2, 1
  ret i32 %ashr
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64-p1:16:16-p2:32:32:32-p3:64:64:64"

define i32 @pr62088() {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %not2 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ -2, %loop ]
  %i.0 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %shr, %loop ]
  %h.0 = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ 1, %loop ]
  %i.0.fr = freeze i32 %i.0
  %sext = shl i32 %i.0.fr, 16
  %conv = ashr exact i32 %sext, 16
  %not = xor i32 %conv, -1
  %and = and i32 %h.0, 1
  %rem.urem = sub nsw i32 %and, %conv
  %rem.cmp = icmp ult i32 %and, %conv
  %rem = select i1 %rem.cmp, i32 %not, i32 %rem.urem
  %xor = xor i32 %rem, %not2
  %sub = sub nsw i32 0, %xor
  %sub5 = sub i32 -1824888657, %xor
  %xor6 = xor i32 %sub5, -1260914025
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %xor6, 824855120
  %shr = ashr i32 %xor6, 40
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %rem
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

@e = external global i8, align 1
@d = external global i32, align 4
@c = external global i32, align 4

define i32 @testoverflowcheck() {
entry:
  %.pr.i = load i8, ptr @e, align 1
  %0 = load i32, ptr @d, align 4
  %c.promoted.i = load i32, ptr @c, align 4
  br label %cond.end.i

cond.end.i:                                       ; preds = %cond.end.i, %entry
  %inc4.i = phi i8 [ %.pr.i, %entry ], [ %inc.i, %cond.end.i ]
  %and3.i = phi i32 [ %c.promoted.i, %entry ], [ %and.i, %cond.end.i ]
  %and.i = and i32 %0, %and3.i
  %inc.i = add i8 %inc4.i, 1
  %tobool.i = icmp eq i8 %inc.i, 0
  br i1 %tobool.i, label %loopexit, label %cond.end.i

loopexit:                                         ; preds = %cond.end.i
  ret i32 %and.i
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:64:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-s0:64:64-f80:128:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @PR32419(i32 %a, i16 %b) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.inc, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ -20, %entry ], [ %i.next, %for.inc ]
  %var0 = phi i32 [ %a, %entry ], [ %var6, %for.inc ]
  %var1 = trunc i32 %i to i16
  %var2 = icmp eq i16 %var1, 0
  br i1 %var2, label %for.inc, label %for.cond

for.cond:                                         ; preds = %for.body
  %var3 = urem i16 %b, %var1
  br label %for.inc

for.inc:                                          ; preds = %for.cond, %for.body
  %var4 = phi i16 [ %var3, %for.cond ], [ 0, %for.body ]
  %var5 = sext i16 %var4 to i32
  %var6 = or i32 %var0, %var5
  %i.next = add nsw i32 %i, 1
  %cond = icmp eq i32 %i.next, 0
  br i1 %cond, label %for.end, label %for.body

for.end:                                          ; preds = %for.inc
  %var7 = phi i32 [ %var6, %for.inc ]
  ret i32 %var7
}

comments.author: imkiva
comments.body:
I tried also `foldOpIntoPhi` for commutative binops, and found [`pr62088` in `select.ll`](https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/8378f1f4cdc8922e4f0409cabff25e0fef517bfa/llvm/test/Transforms/InstCombine/select.ll#L3585) was simplified to
```
; CHECK-LABEL: define i32 @pr62088() {
; CHECK-NEXT:  entry:
; CHECK-NEXT:    br label %[[LOOP:.*]]
; CHECK:       [[LOOP]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    br i1 true, label %[[LOOP]], label %[[EXIT:.*]]
; CHECK:       [[EXIT]]:
; CHECK-NEXT:    ret i32 poison
```

I am checking if this was correct, because alive2 times out for this case

comments.body: I added 4 testcases with binop(w/swapped)+phi(w/swapped) covering all execution paths of `foldBinOpIntoSelectOrPhi`

comments.author: maurer
comments.body:
This change breaks the Rust test for [branchless ASCII classification](https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/blob/b90dc1e597db0bbc0cab0eccb39747b1a9d7e607/tests/codegen-llvm/char-ascii-branchless.rs), with output like

```
 --- stderr -------------------------------
rust/tests/codegen-llvm/char-ascii-branchless.rs:38:16: error: CHECK-NOT: excluded string found in input
 // CHECK-NOT: br {{.*}}
               ^
rust/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/codegen-llvm/char-ascii-branchless/char-ascii-branchless.ll:64:2: note: found here
 br i1 %_8.i, label %bb6.i, label %_RNvMNtNtCscGzNLIcHm04_4core4char7methodsc20is_ascii_punctuation.exit
 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
rust/tests/codegen-llvm/char-ascii-branchless.rs:45:16: error: CHECK-NOT: excluded string found in input
 // CHECK-NOT: br {{.*}}
               ^
rust/build/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/test/codegen-llvm/char-ascii-branchless/char-ascii-branchless.ll:88:2: note: found here
 br i1 %_8.i, label %bb6.i, label %_RNvMs4_NtCscGzNLIcHm04_4core3numh20is_ascii_punctuation.exit
 ^~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
 ```
 
We haven't yet investigated what's going on here, but I figured I'd post this in case one of you two immediately knew what was going on already :)
